unit07.title: Unit 7 - AI and Machine Learning
unit07.summary: Hands-on intro to training models with tabular data. Students explore how computers learn from data, then build and ship an AI-powered app for a real problem.
unit07.description: This unit introduces artificial intelligence through practical machine learning with tabular data. Students learn core ideas—patterns, classification vs. numeric prediction, training data, features, labels, and model evaluation—while also examining bias and ethical impact. They use AI Lab to train models and App Lab to build interfaces that use those models. The unit culminates in a team project: designing, training, and publishing an AI app that addresses a personally relevant issue.

chapters.description: Understanding Machine Learning: foundational concepts, patterns in data, classification, model documentation, and importing models into apps.
chapters.goal01: Describe how models learn patterns from features to predict labels.
chapters.goal02: Differentiate classification and numerical prediction tasks.
chapters.goal03: Train and evaluate models in AI Lab and import them into App Lab.
chapters.goal04: Document decisions and limitations using Model Cards and discuss ethics and bias.
chapters.goal05: Customize multi-screen apps that use trained models.
chapters.big questions 01: How do computers learn from data to make predictions or classifications?
chapters.big questions 02: What choices do we make when training models, and how do we evaluate them fairly?
chapters.big questions 03: How do we embed a model inside an app and design a good user experience?

lessons.title: Intro to App Lab (Optional)
lessons.overview: Quick refresher on App Lab UI and events. Create simple screens and interactions; extend with a small starter app.
lessons.question: How can we quickly build and share an interactive app in App Lab?
lessons.content: Create screens and UI elements; add events; test and share a simple app.
lessons.objective1: Navigate App Lab and use design mode to add UI elements.
lessons.objective2: Write basic event-driven code to connect UI to behavior.
lessons.objective3: Share and iterate on a small app prototype.

lessons.title: Introduction to Machine Learning
lessons.overview: Use the Problem Solving Process to guide a robot (A.I. Bot) through pattern recognition activities (fish examples).
lessons.question: How can we use the Problem Solving Process to solve a problem with machine learning?
lessons.content: Explore pattern recognition tasks and reflect on define → prepare → try → reflect.
lessons.objective1: Explain what a machine learning model is at a high level.
lessons.objective2: Relate the Problem Solving Process to training and testing.
lessons.objective3: Identify patterns and features that support predictions.

lessons.title: Types of Machine Learning
lessons.overview: Contrast supervised and unsupervised learning with interactive games and scenarios.
lessons.question: What are different types of machine learning?
lessons.content: Play 'Green Glass Door' and sort scenarios into supervised vs. unsupervised.
lessons.objective1: Differentiate supervised vs. unsupervised learning.
lessons.objective2: Describe mental models and model building from examples.
lessons.objective3: Match problem types to appropriate ML approaches.

lessons.title: Innovations in AI
lessons.overview: Explore real AI applications (e.g., Seeing AI) and research impacts on users and society; share findings.
lessons.question: How is AI helping to solve problems around the world?
lessons.content: Investigate examples, identify users impacted, and present takeaways.
lessons.objective1: Describe a real AI system and its intended users.
lessons.objective2: Identify benefits and potential harms of AI systems.
lessons.objective3: Communicate findings to peers.

lessons.title: Patterns in Data
lessons.overview: Analyze shoe recommendation scenarios to see how models use patterns to make decisions; design a short survey.
lessons.question: What strategies do computer models use to make decisions?
lessons.content: Compare simple recommenders; design and interpret a small survey for improved decisions.
lessons.objective1: Explain features, labels, and patterns in tabular data.
lessons.objective2: Design survey questions aligned to a prediction goal.
lessons.objective3: Reflect on patterns and decision quality.

lessons.title: Classification Models
lessons.overview: Unplugged simulation: separate fruits and vegetables using 2D feature space and boundaries.
lessons.question: How do computers learn to classify data?
lessons.content: Graph examples, test boundaries, and reason about decision regions.
lessons.objective1: Represent data points with features and labels.
lessons.objective2: Explain how a classifier separates groups.
lessons.objective3: Evaluate classification choices and accuracy at the boundary.

lessons.title: Introduction to AI Lab
lessons.overview: First use of AI Lab: select features, train a model, and inspect visualizations for relationships.
lessons.question: How can we use machine learning to make recommendations?
lessons.content: Train models (shapes, pizza, restaurant data) and choose useful features from visualizations.
lessons.objective1: Train a basic classification model with chosen features.
lessons.objective2: Use data visualizations to inform feature selection.
lessons.objective3: Interpret metrics to assess model quality.

lessons.title: Importing Models in App Lab
lessons.overview: Bring an AI Lab model into App Lab; connect it to UI and events; customize a starter recommender app.
lessons.question: How can I create an app using machine learning?
lessons.content: Import a model, add a welcome screen, and wire events for predictions.
lessons.objective1: Import and use a trained model in App Lab.
lessons.objective2: Implement event-driven predictions tied to form inputs.
lessons.objective3: Customize UI and flow for usability.

lessons.title: Model Cards
lessons.overview: Investigate bias using a Medical Priority example; write Model Cards to document data, decisions, and limitations.
lessons.question: How can we evaluate machine learning models once they've been trained?
lessons.content: Examine bias, practice writing Model Cards for sample and own models.
lessons.objective1: Identify sources of model bias and limitations.
lessons.objective2: Draft a Model Card that communicates key context.
lessons.objective3: Use documentation to inform ethical use in apps.

lessons.title: Saving Models in AI Lab
lessons.overview: Choose a label to predict, select features, train and save a model with a Model Card, then import to App Lab.
lessons.question: How can I use Model Cards to document my decisions when training a model?
lessons.content: Train, save, document, import, and begin customizing an app with your model.
lessons.objective1: Save a trained model and associated Model Card.
lessons.objective2: Import the model into App Lab and test predictions.
lessons.objective3: Explain training choices using the Model Card.

lessons.title: Model Cards in App Lab
lessons.overview: Use Model Cards inside App Lab to improve forms, guidance, and UX; extend with additional screens and styles.
lessons.question: How can I use a Model Card to improve my app?
lessons.content: View Model Card in-app; add explanatory copy and styles; practice improvements.
lessons.objective1: Surface model info to guide user inputs and expectations.
lessons.objective2: Add UX affordances that reduce misuse and confusion.
lessons.objective3: Organize multi-screen flows that use the same model.

lessons.title: Numerical Models
lessons.overview: Unplugged zombie-outbreak simulation to predict numeric values using similarity and averages; compare accuracy notions.
lessons.question: How do computers learn to make predictions with numerical data?
lessons.content: Use degrees of similarity and averaging to predict numeric labels; compare to true values.
lessons.objective1: Explain numeric prediction vs. classification.
lessons.objective2: Use similarity/averaging ideas to make predictions.
lessons.objective3: Discuss accuracy metrics for numeric tasks.

lessons.title: Numerical Data in AI Lab
lessons.overview: Train models with numerical features/labels; use new visualization tools to find relationships and evaluate performance.
lessons.question: How can we use AI Lab to predict numerical data?
lessons.content: Explore a safari dataset; choose features; train and evaluate a numeric model.
lessons.objective1: Use AI Lab tools for numeric prediction tasks.
lessons.objective2: Interpret visualizations for continuous variables.
lessons.objective3: Assess and compare numeric model performance.

lessons.title: Customizing Apps
lessons.overview: Expand a one-screen model app into a multi-screen experience; update code for new UI elements and flows.
lessons.question: How can I customize the code for a machine learning app?
lessons.content: Build Raspado Recommender and Driver Alert examples; improve your own app.
lessons.objective1: Refactor code to support multi-screen navigation.
lessons.objective2: Integrate new UI elements with existing prediction logic.
lessons.objective3: Improve UX through layout, copy, and flow changes.

lessons.title: AI Code of Ethics
lessons.overview: Research ethical pitfalls in AI and co-create a class AI Code of Ethics with solution-oriented principles.
lessons.question: What guidelines can we use to create ethical machine learning apps?
lessons.content: Review articles/videos; propose principles; assemble a shared code of ethics.
lessons.objective1: Identify ethical risks and stakeholders for AI systems.
lessons.objective2: Propose actionable, responsible design principles.
lessons.objective3: Synthesize principles into a class resource.

lessons.title: Project — Make a Machine Learning App
lessons.overview: Mini-project using real-world datasets: train, document, and build a simple app powered by your model.
lessons.question: Can I use real-world data to create an app that uses machine learning?
lessons.content: Choose a dataset; train and save a model with a Model Card; build a working app.
lessons.objective1: Select features that meaningfully improve accuracy.
lessons.objective2: Import the model and implement predictions in-app.
lessons.objective3: Explain decisions and limitations in documentation.

chapters.description: Design a Machine Learning App: move from an issue to data collection, model training, troubleshooting, and shipping a polished AI app.
chapters.goal01: Plan an issue-focused AI app grounded in real user needs and data.
chapters.goal02: Design and run a survey; clean and import data; train and evaluate models.
chapters.goal03: Integrate models into multi-screen App Lab apps with strong UX.
chapters.goal04: Troubleshoot datasets and models; iterate ethically and responsibly.
chapters.goal05: Present process and product, reflecting on impact and next steps.
chapters.big questions 01: How can machine learning address a problem in our community?
chapters.big questions 02: How do we collect, clean, and use data responsibly?
chapters.big questions 03: How do we build, test, and present an AI app as a team?

lessons.title: Issue Statements
lessons.overview: Use 5 Whys to brainstorm issues and evaluate ideas for an AI solution, mirroring the final project workflow.
lessons.question: How can machine learning be used to address an issue in your community?
lessons.content: Brainstorm and refine issue statements; consider feasibility and impact.
lessons.objective1: Develop clear, scoped statements of a community issue.
lessons.objective2: Evaluate issues for ML suitability and ethical impact.
lessons.objective3: Select a direction for the final project.

lessons.title: Survey Planning
lessons.overview: Design a survey for a recommender (club example): write useful questions and build a Google Form.
lessons.question: How can I create a survey to gather data for a machine learning app?
lessons.content: Draft questions; create a Form; plan for data quality and privacy.
lessons.objective1: Write survey items aligned to target predictions.
lessons.objective2: Plan for data cleaning and consistent formats.
lessons.objective3: Consider privacy, consent, and transparency.

lessons.title: Survey Data in AI Lab
lessons.overview: Export responses to CSV, upload to AI Lab, explore, and train; practice cleaning another student's messy dataset.
lessons.question: How can I import data into AI Lab to train a machine learning model?
lessons.content: Move from Sheets to CSV; upload and visualize; fix errors; train a model.
lessons.objective1: Import and explore tabular survey data in AI Lab.
lessons.objective2: Identify and fix common data issues before training.
lessons.objective3: Train an initial model and record decisions.

lessons.title: Troubleshooting Models
lessons.overview: Analyze why models fail and develop strategies to avoid or mitigate issues (missing data, weak features, imbalance).
lessons.question: What are strategies to make sure our data generates an accurate model?
lessons.content: Compare alternate datasets; iterate on features and data quality.
lessons.objective1: Diagnose data/model issues through targeted checks.
lessons.objective2: Revise surveys, features, or sampling to improve results.
lessons.objective3: Document troubleshooting strategies for future use.

lessons.title: Creating an App
lessons.overview: Import the club recommender into App Lab and customize: welcome screen, feature descriptions, and UI polish.
lessons.question: How can I create a friendly, easy-to-use machine learning app?
lessons.content: Import model; connect screens and events; customize copy and layout.
lessons.objective1: Integrate model predictions into a coherent multi-screen app.
lessons.objective2: Write clear guidance so users enter valid inputs.
lessons.objective3: Polish usability and style for the target audience.

lessons.title: Project — Design an AI App
lessons.overview: Final project: collect data, train a model and write a Model Card, then build and present an AI app.
lessons.question: How can I create an AI app that solves a problem in my community?
lessons.content: Prepare data and train in AI Lab; build in App Lab; peer review, revise, and present.
lessons.objective1: Ship a functional AI app that uses a trained model.
lessons.objective2: Document choices, limitations, and ethics via a Model Card.
lessons.objective3: Reflect on impact and next steps after feedback.
